message:
test(core): property-based tests for suggestions module (wa-d4tg)

68 property tests covering levenshtein_distance (identity/symmetry/
triangle inequality/bounds), suggest_closest (exact/case/prefix/empty),
format_available (truncation at 10), PaneInfo/StateChange/UserHistory/
FeatureHint/SystemMetrics (builder preservation), Platform (Display/
install commands), Priority (ordering), SuggestionType (Display),
SuggestionId (Display/clone), Suggestion (builder/dismissable/action),
DismissedStore (permanent/temporary/count), SuggestionEngine (disabled/
max/priority sorting/dismissed exclusion), SuggestionContext (suggest_pane/
workflow/rule, format methods), pane_not_found/workflow_not_found helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.7.4","title":"Implement performance regression suite with strict CI quality gates","description":"## Context
Performance regressions must be blocked automatically before merge and before release promotion.

## Scope
Implement benchmark and e2e performance gates for resize/reflow behavior, including artifact and interaction-latency checks where applicable.

## Deliverables
- CI job(s) with deterministic benchmark execution and threshold checks
- Baseline management workflow (refresh policy + audit trail)
- Regression classification output (hard fail vs warning with rationale)
- Machine-readable reports for frame pacing, interaction latency, and resource usage

## Why this matters
Objective automated gates prevent subjective performance regressions from reaching users.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails on threshold violations with actionable diagnostics.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","title":"Define no-regression compatibility contract for terminal semantics and user workflows","description":"## Context
Resize improvements are only successful if terminal semantics and common workflows remain correct.

## Scope
Define the no-regression compatibility contract as release policy, covering cursor behavior, wrapping semantics, scrollback integrity, alt-screen behavior, and interaction continuity.

## Deliverables
- Compatibility contract document with explicit invariant list
- Mapping from invariants to validation suites and artifact evidence
- Escalation process when contract checks fail

## Why this matters
This protects users from subtle behavioral regressions masked by performance gains.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract enumerates all critical user-visible semantics and invariants.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.8.6","title":"Execute final go/no-go review and archive evidence bundle for sign-off","description":"## Context

- Rollback plan, kill-switch verification, and on-call readiness are confirmed.
- Decision record references beta feedback and SLO compliance  {"id":"wa-1u9qw","title":"Protocol-versioned local IPC namespace and compatibility handshake","description":"Goal: adopt contract-versioned local socket namespace and explicit compatibility handshake for FrankenTerm local IPC (robot and native). Why: prevent client/server skew from attaching to incompatible sessions. Scope: version local IPC endpoints by protocol or schema version, add handshake with explicit mismatch error payload (expected and actual version), define compatibility policy, and add integration tests for mismatch and upgrade. Deliverables: IPC versioning spec, endpoint discovery implementation changes, and compatibility tests with operator-visible diagnostics. Acceptance criteria: incompatible clients rejected deterministically with stable error code; compatible clients connect without behavior regressions; robot and native workflows keep stable semantics across patch upgrades. Cross-references: wa-vcjbi, wa-3kxe, wa-dr6zv, wa-3dfxb.13.","notes":"wa-1u9qw implementation now fully re-applied and validated in workspace.

1) crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
- Added protocol constants: IPC_CONTRACT_DIR_NAME=contract_version_1, IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION=1, IPC_PROTOCOL_MISMATCH_CODE=ipc.protocol_mismatch.
- Added IpcEnvelope.protocol_version with serde default for legacy requests.
- Server rejects protocol mismatch or missing protocol version with stable code ipc.protocol_mismatch and expected vs actual message.
- Auth failures now return stable code ipc.auth_denied.
- Client includes protocol_version in every IPC envelope.
- Added tests: ipc_envelope_includes_protocol_version and ipc_rejects_protocol_mismatch.
2) crates/frankenterm-core/src/config.rs
- Default ipc.socket_path now uses IPC_SOCKET_NAME constant.
- Relative IPC socket paths now resolve under .ft/contract_version_1.
- Added tests for namespaced relative path and absolute-path passthrough.
3) crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs
- IPC pane-state helper now surfaces error_code with detail in returned error string.

- rustfmt --edition 2024 on changed files: pass.
